lede: A look at August 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Victoria Jiménez Kasintseva and Marek Niit, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Voepass Flight 2283
events.desc: Voepass Linhas Aéreas Flight 2283 crashes near Vinhedo, São Paulo, killing all 62 people on board.

events.title: 2006 transatlantic aircraft plot
events.desc: At least 21 suspected terrorists are arrested in the 2006 transatlantic aircraft plot that happened in the United Kingdom. The arrests are made in London, Birmingham, and High Wycombe in an overnight operation.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: In Northern Ireland, the British authorities launch Operation Demetrius. The operation involves the mass arrest and internment without trial of individuals suspected of being affiliated with the Irish Republican Army (PIRA). Mass riots follow, and thousands of people flee or are forced out of their homes.

events.title: LANSA Flight 502
events.desc: LANSA Flight 502 crashes after takeoff from Alejandro Velasco Astete International Airport in Cusco, Peru, killing 99 of the 100 people on board, as well as two people on the ground.

events.title: United States Forest Service
events.desc: The United States Forest Service and the Wartime Advertising Council release posters featuring Smokey Bear for the first time.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of Savo Island: Allied naval forces protecting their amphibious forces during the initial stages of the Battle of Guadalcanal are surprised and defeated by an Imperial Japanese Navy cruiser force.

events.title: International Congress of Mathematicians
events.desc: The first International Congress of Mathematicians is held in Zürich, Switzerland.

events.title: American Indian Wars
events.desc: American Indian Wars: Battle of the Big Hole: A small band of Nez Percé Indians clash with the United States Army.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Cedar Mountain: At Cedar Mountain, Virginia, Confederate General Stonewall Jackson narrowly defeats Union forces under General John Pope.

births.name: J. James Exon
births.line: American soldier and politician, 33rd Governor of Nebraska (died 2005)

deaths.text: Eoin McNamee (Irish republican), Chief of Staff of the Irish Republican Army

observances.text: Christian feast day: Candida Maria of Jesus
Christian feast day: Edith Stein (St Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)
Christian feast day: Herman of Alaska (Russian Orthodox Church and related congregations; Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: John Vianney (1950s – currently August 4)
Christian feast day: Mary Sumner (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Nath Í of Achonry
